widgets_values:
These are good resolutions to gen in. They are all divisible by 16.

Note: the largest 16:9 resolution is slightly off (1088 instead of 1080) in order to be divisible by 16. Remember to cut the extra 8 pixels off afterwards if you need perfect 16:9.

nodes.title: Important Loras
widgets_values:
**TURBO LORA**

Use the raw main model, and always leave the turbo lora on at 0.6 strength.

**2 VECTOR FILTER BYPASS LORA**

Set it to 1.0 strength for most things. Leave it on even when you're using NSFW loras (but don't go higher than 1.0 then).

It will degrade image quality past 1.0 strength, which is why you usually don't want to go higher.

You can also use NSFW loras to improve prompt adherence *even for SFW prompts*.

New finetunes and loras will probably help in the future.

nodes.title: Models
widgets_values:
## Main Model

Krea 2 RAW.

[Bf16 & FP8 versions](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Krea-2/tree/main/diffusion_models)

[GGUF versions](https://huggingface.co/vantagewithai/Krea-2-Raw-GGUF/tree/main)

Use the RAW model for this workflow, not the turbo model.

Goes in models/diffusion_models

## Turbo Lora

[Rank 64 Turbo Lora](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Krea-2/blob/main/loras/krea2_turbo_lora_rank_64_bf16.safetensors)

Required when using the RAW model. Set it to 0.6 strength for this workflow.

Goes in models/loras

## Anti-Censorship Lora

[2 Vector Bypass Lora](https://civitai.com/models/2728234/krea2filterbypass?modelVersionId=3066812)

Required if you want the model to work properly, even for SFW prompts. Set it to only 1.0 strength most of the time, but sometimes you may need to set it higher (2-6) if the model keeps refusing to do what you ask. This is a thing even for SFW prompts, especially when you want strong facial expressions.

Goes in models/loras

## Text Encoder

[Qwen 3 VL 4B](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Krea-2/tree/main/text_encoders)

Use BF16 if you can.

Goes in models/text_encoders

## VAE

[Wan 2.1 FP32 VAE (RECOMMENDED)](https://huggingface.co/Kijai/WanVideo_comfy/blob/main/Wan2_1_VAE_fp32.safetensors)

The FP32 Wan VAE gives sharp results and has no downsides.


[Wan 2.1 Upscale2x VAE (OPTIONAL)](https://huggingface.co/spacepxl/Wan2.1-VAE-upscale2x/blob/main/Wan2.1_VAE_upscale2x_imageonly_real_v1.safetensors)

This gives even sharper results than the regular Wan 2.1 FP32 VAE, but it also introduces noise & artifacts to the image.

To use it, you need the VAE-Utils Comfy node set installed so you can use the 'VAE Decode (VAE Utils)' node instead of the normal vae decoder. You also need to downscale your image by 0.5 afterwards (the VAE doubles the resolution).

[Qwen Image VAE (OPTIONAL)](https://huggingface.co/Comfy-Org/Krea-2/tree/main/vae)

The qwen image VAE is the 'normal' one for Krea 2. It gives blurrier results than the Wan 2.1 FP32 VAE, but you may prefer it this way if you plan to post-process your images.

Goes in models/vae

nodes.title: Negative Prompts?
widgets_values:
You can use negative prompts if you're using the RAW main model with the turbo lora.

To do this, you need to set the CFG to ~2.0 strength. This will 'enable' CFG which makes negative prompts work, but be aware that this will **double** the time it takes to generate an image.
If you're using a GGUF model, swap the model loader node to a "Unet Loader (GGUF)" node instead. You need the 'ComfyUI-GGUF' custom nodes installed to do that.
If you're using a GGUF model, swap the model loader node to a "Unet Loader (GGUF)" node instead. You need the 'ComfyUI-GGUF' custom nodes installed to do that.
